summary.recommendation: Use hxrust for sidecar/helper and selected adapter slices only; keep the Cafex fork path for unsupported live runtime seams until HXCX-6.3 accepts a narrower replacement gate.
summary.rebaseFrictionClaim: Evidence supports reduced upstream-rebase friction only for fixture-backed supported rows. Unsupported live runtime rows still require native fork patches or future generic hxrust capability work.

pathAreas.maintenanceSignal: session, runtime, state, tools, registry, and native conformance tests
live composer, queue, plan checkpoint, slash command, status, and UI tests
Cargo lock plus adjacent crates touched by runtime composition

classification.friction: native schema advertisement and receipt semantics must be replayed against upstream runtime changes
live composer, pending-input, and checkpoint hooks sit in high-churn TUI paths
Caf request/receipt semantics cross state, session handling, and user-visible control surfaces
upstream sync policy is review evidence rather than runtime behavior

burdenSignals: Wrapper semantics move out of the Codex Rust fork, but become Haxe implementation, fixture, and harness maintenance.
Generated Rust is intentionally disposable; parity must be proven by Haxe fixtures and generated Cargo gates rather than committed generated output.
Unsupported live runtime rows remain explicit so hxrust cannot silently claim replacement coverage.

comparison.supportedSliceResult: For receipt writing, continuity metadata, active-lane capability advertisement, effort/wake/mode/goal/queue-reconcile bridge fixtures, and minimal goal lifecycle, hxrust can reduce Codex upstream-rebase friction by replacing fork replay with Haxe fixture and generator gates.
comparison.unsupportedSliceResult: For live mode/restart, plan checkpoint guard, and live TUI/model runtime, hxrust does not yet reduce fork friction because no generic runtime ownership exists.
comparison.netResult: The hxrust path is maintenance-positive for selected adapter slices and review tooling, but not yet a broad Cafex replacement.
decisionInputs: HXCX-6.3 should evaluate a sidecar/helper or selected-adapter-slice go/no-go, not a full production replacement.
Any future replacement claim must first reduce the 4 unsupported rows, account for live queue mutation, or explicitly decide those surfaces stay native.
haxe.rust limitations discovered while expanding unsupported rows should be filed in Beads and fixed generically, never as codexhx-specific compiler behavior.
